def: s.m. Bagging, bellying out; pendulousness; incurvation; curve (as of a rope hanging freely between two points of suspension); a bag, wallet, knapsack; bending or waving (the hand, in beckoning), beckoning (with the hand); — palsy, paralysis; — a hot wind or blast; a stroke of the sun; a cold blighting wind (which withers or dries up the ears of wheat); — adj. Loose, slack, bagging, baggy; pendulous; curved.

ety: S. झौलिका; prob. a Prk. form fr. rt. व्हल्
def: s.f. A bag, sack, pouch, wallet; — pendulous belly (of an ox, &c.); — a sheet used as a fan in winnowing ( = jhūlī, q.v.); a trick in wrestling
subs: v.n. To get or have a pendulous belly, to grow fat
adj. Furnished with a bag or pouch; (in Bot. and Anat.) saccate.

ety: jhol˚ = S. व्हालय(ति), rt. व्हल्; see jhūlnā
def: v.t. To move or swing to and fro (as a fan, &c.); to shake, put in motion, stir, agitate (as water, &c.); — s.m. A bag ( = jholā, jholī).
v.t. To cause to be soldered or cemented, to get (a thing) soldered or repaired.
v.t. (dialec.), To set in motion or action, to set agoing, to get up; to apply; to beat, strike.
s.m. Name of a division of the Kurmī tribe.
s.m. = jhojh, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) = jhaunjh, q.v.
s.f. = jhok, q.v.
s.m. = jhokā, q.v.; — (also) bunch or cluster (of fruit); ear or beard (of corn).
v.t. = jhukānā, jhoṅknā, q.v.
s.m. The keeping safe of property; — the charge for securing property from accidents, insurance ( = jokhim; bīmā).
s.m. = jhokiyā, q.v.
v.t. = jhoknā, q.v.

ety: Prk. झुंपओ; S. क्षुम्प+कः; cf. khoṅpā
def: s.m. A cluster or bunch (of fruit), a spike; a coil (of hair on the top of the head), top-knot ( = jhoṅṭā); — a cloth with eyelets which covers the whole body.

ety: Prk. झुंपडओ, and झुपडिआ; S. झुम्प+र+क and इका
def: s.f. (dim.) [Prk. झुंपडओ, and झुपडिआ; S. झुम्प+र+क and इका], A small hut or hovel built of reeds and mud; cottage, cot, hut; a thatched roof or shed.

ety: Prk. झुंपडओ, and झुपडिआ; S. झुम्प+र+क and इका
def: s.f. (dim.) = H جهونپڙا झोंपड़ा jhoṅpṛā, s.m. [Prk. झुंपडओ, and झुपडिआ; S. झुम्प+र+क and इका], A small hut or hovel built of reeds and mud; cottage, cot, hut; a thatched roof or shed.
s.m. A bunch or cluster (of fruit, = jhoṅpā); — also = jholā, q.v.

ety: S. झुण्ट+कः, or जुटकः; rt. जुट्
def: s.m. Coil (of a woman's hair), top-knot ( = jhoṅpā; khoṅpā); a buffalo (so called on account of its hump); — swing, oscillation; the motion of a swing; nodding; — jhoṅṭā denā (-ko), To shake (the head); to nod
subs: jhoṭam-jhāṭā, s.m. Mutual pulling and tugging at the hair of the head.

def: s.f. Coil (of a woman's hair); — a young buffalo.
s.m. = H جهوپڙي झोपड़ी jhopṛī, s.f. See jhoṅpṛā, and jhoṅpṛī.
s.f. = H جهوپڙا झोपड़ा jhopṛā, s.m. See jhoṅpṛā, and jhoṅpṛī.
s.m. The haulm or stalk of a leguminous plant (such as mūṅg, moṭh, &c. — used as fodder).
v.t. To shake (fruit off a tree); to beat, to thrash ( = jhārnā); — to pound, grind (cf. jhauṛnā & jholnā).
s.m. = jhoṅṭā, q.v.
s.m. = jhūpā; ḍhūʼā, qq.v.
adj. & s.m. Unoccupied, idle; — one who wastes time, an idler, a trifler.
s.m. = jhūʼā, q.v.
v.n. = jhuṅjhlānā, q.v.
v.n. = jūjhnā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Bent, stooping, inclined; (in Bot.) decumbent

ety: caus. of jhuknā; — jhuk˚+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि;+n ā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To cause to stoop, to bend downwards, to tilt; to cause to nod; to incline (the head), to nod, bow; to abash; to lower (as a gun, in order to take aim); to put into a scale, to weigh; to speak (at), direct speech at, -par)

ety: jhukā(nā)+āʼo or āw = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: s.m. The state of being bent downward; stoop; bend, inclination; curve; irregular line; (in Mech.) flexibility
s.f. Bending downwards, stooping; nodding; the state of being bent downward; bend; inclination.

ety: jhuk˚ = Prk. झुक्क(इ), fr. S. क्षुभ (acc. sing. n. क्षुप)+कृ
def: v.n. To be bent down (as the bough of a tree), to bend, stoop, lean, incline; to be tilted; to preponderate, to dip (as a scale, or the arm of a balance); to bow, to nod; to crouch; to be prone (to, -meṅ), be ready (for); to hang down, to drop (as the eyes); to be abashed; to stoop (to, -se), pay homage (to), humble oneself (before); to submit, to yield; to be angry; to be perplexed; to be spent, be thrown away (in or on, -meṅ)
subs: jhuk-jānā, v.n. intensives of and = jhuknā.

def: s.f. Affliction, suffering, grief, sorrow.
part. adj. (f. -ī), Bending, stooping, &c. (see jhuknā)
subs: adj. & adv. Crouching and dodging (to escape observation, &c.).

def: s.f. A swing ( = jhūlā; jhūlnā); body-clothes (of cattle, &c.), horse-cloth, housings ( = A. jul); a bag, wallet ( = jholā).
s.m. A swinging-rope, a swing; a cradle; — a species of song sung when swinging
subs: (-meṅ), To put up a swinging-rope or a swing.

def: s.f. Swinging, oscillating, &c.
subs: (cf. S. dolana+yātrā), s.f. A Hindū festival kept about new moon in August in honour of Kr̤ishṇa and his mistress Rādhā. (Idols representing these personages are placed in a cradle or chair suspended from the roof of the temple or house and swung to and fro while offerings of fruits, &c. are presented. The Brahmans are feasted, and love-songs of the most indecent description are chanted all night. This festival is usually celebrated for five days and nights.)

def: v.t. To cause to swing or sway; to swing, to push a swing (in which others are), to give (one) a swing; to rock (a cradle); to make (one, as a dun) dance after one; to dangle, hang
subs: v.t. To keep one running to and fro; to keep one (at, -meṅ, a thing, as reading, &c.).

def: s.m. Swinging, waving, swaying from side to side; wavy motion (as of trees, water, &c.), oscillation.

v.n. To be burnt, be scorched, be singed, be charred, be parched, be seared, be branded ( = bhulasnā); — v.t. To scorch, singe, &c.; (met.) to blacken (the face), to disgrace (by getting one to accept a bribe, &c.)
subs: v.t. To scorch, burn, singe, &c. = jhulsānā.

def: s.f. A cloth or sheet waved to and fro as a fan for winnowing grain when there is no wind ( = jholī); — a swinging oot, a hammock.
v.t. To burn, to scorch, to singe ( = jhulasnā).
s.m. A shirt or frock that covers the body from the shoulders downward (syn. jhangā).
s.f. A wrinkle, fold, plait (in cloth), a crease.
s.m. = jhurmaṭ, q.v.
v.n. = jhūlnā, q.v.

ety: jhūl˚ = S. व्हल(ति), rt. व्हल् (orig. ध्वल्
def: v.n. To hang down, to dangle; to swing, sway to and fro, to oscillate, rock, reel; to swing (for amusement or exercise); — s.m. A swing; a cradle; a kind of poem or song.
part. adj. (f. -ī), Scorched, singed, charred, seared, branded.
v.t. To cause to singe; to have singed; — to scorch, to singe, to sear, to brand.

ety: rt. of jhūl-nā+S. अ+कः (with व inserted)
def: s.m. A wreath or garland of flowers.
s.m. Swaying, waving, undulating; abundance of foliage
subs: s.m. A gathering of clouds; — adv. Crowding, in dense crowds, thickly; overpoweringly (as sleep, &c.)

def: s.f. A dense mass, a crowd, multitude, an assembly, company, a ball; array (of troops); battle, engagement ( = jhurmaṭ).

s.m. A gathering, assembly, company, band; a company of women dancing; a kind of dance (in which the dancers join hands, syn. dast-band); a kind of song sung by women in concert; — an ornament consisting of a number of chains forming a fringe, which is attached to the top-knot (of a woman's head) and falls on the forehead.
s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode (cf. jhūmar, and jhūmnā).
adj. Shaking, swaying, waving; moving (the head) up and down (as quadrupeds).
s.f. = jhumari, q.v.
s.m. A bunch or cluster (of flowers or fruit); a tuft, a tassel; the bell-shaped pendant (of an ear-ring); the passion-flower; the flower Gloriosa superba; Charles's Wain, Ursa Major; the Pleiades
s.m. = H جهومکي झूमकी jhūmkī, s.f. = jhumkā, jhumkī, qq.v.
s.f. A kind of ear-ring with a pendant.
s.f. = H جهومکا झूमका jhūmkā, s.m. = jhumkā, jhumkī, qq.v.

ety: prob. fr. S. क्षुभ्; cf. jhūlnā
def: v.n. To shake, to sway to and fro or from side to side, to wave; to stagger, roll, reel; to move with stately steps, to strut; to move (the head) up and down (as an animal); to nod (through sleep); to hang loosely, hang down; to droop; to gather, to lower (as clouds).
s.f. (dialec.), Name of a Hindū festival.
s.f. A slight resemblance.

ety: S. जूर्ण, rt. जुर्, or जीर्ण, rt. ज्ऋ+कं
def: s.m. A ripe cocoa-nut; — a kind of muslin (syn. malmal).

ety: Prk.-S. झुण्टः, prob. fr. S. जुटः or जूटः
def: s.m. A shrub, bush; underwood; a tuft (of grass, &c.); a clump (of trees); a thicket; thick hair, mop (of hair); a ragged-looking tree.

ety: Prk. जुत्थं; S. यूथं; but cf. the preceding
def: s.m. A multitude, crowd, troop; a flock, herd; a swarm; — the buzzing noise of a crowd
s.m. A heap; a crowd; an association (see jhuṅḍ).

ety: jhunḍ, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A bush; a tuft (of grass); thicket, jungle; the root of grain stalks, sugar-cane, &c.
s.f. A lot or parcel of land in a coparcenary village; the amount due from each sharer in a coparcenary estate; — a clump of grass, &c. ( = jhunḍī, q.v.).
s.f. A lot or parcel of land in a coparcenary village; the amount due from each sharer in a coparcenary estate; — a clump of grass, &c. ( = jhunḍī, q.v.).
s.m. = jhūṅgā, q.v.
s.m. Bramble, brushwood ( = jhāṅgī).

ety: fr. jhukā, perf. part. of jhuknā
def: s.m. (f. -ī), An ox or bullock with horns bent downward; — jhūṅgī, s.f. Long, downward-hanging moustache.
s.f. = jhūṅjhal, q.v.
s.m. Nest (of a bird); &c. = jhojh, q.v.
adj. Petulant, peevish; discontented, dissatisfied.
s.f. Access of anger, rage, vexation, petulance, snappishness, irritableness, peevishness.
s.f. Petulance, irritation, snappishness, peevishness, raging, storming.
adv. In irritation, petulantly, angrily, snappishly (cf. jhunjhliyā).
v.n. = jhuṅjhlānā, q.v.

ety: jhunjhal˚, prob. Prk. झुणि redupl.; S. ध्वनि redupl.
def: v.n. To be petulant or irritable, to snap (at); to rage, storm; to be peevish or fretful.
s.f. = jhunjhlāhaṭ, q.v.

ety: rt. of jhunjhlānā+iyā = Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: adj. Irritable, petulant, snappish, cross, peevish.

ety: Prk. झुणिओ redupl.; S. ध्वनि redupl.+कः
def: s.m. A child's rattle.
s.f. A small bell (one of such as are worn on the feet); (fig.) an iron fetter (so called from its clanking or rattling).
v.n. = jhunjhlānā, q.v.
s.m. = jhunjhunā, q.v.
s.m. A bundle of twigs ( = jhur-kaṭ, q.v.).
v.n. = jhuknā, q.v.
v.n. (prov.) = jhūmnā, q.v.
s.m. A fine kind of muslin ( = jhūnā, q.v.).
s.m. A shrub, bush, bramble ( = jhuṛ).
s.m. = jhūṭh, q.v.
s.f. Land yielding a double crop yearly ( = jhūṭhan, q.v.)
s.m. Leavings of food, &c. = jhūṭhan, q.v.
s.f. Land yielding a double crop yearly ( = jhūṭhan, q.v.)

ety: S. जूट or जुट+इका, or dim. of jhoṭā
def: s.f. A lock of hair left at the back or top of the head when the rest is shaven.
s.m. A collection, heap, pile (as of fruit, corn-stalks, &c.; cf. ḍhūʼā).
s.m. = jhūpā; jhoṅpā, q.v.
s.m. A bush, bramble ( = jhūṛ).

ety: S. जुट् or जूट; cf. झूण्टः
def: s.m. A bush, a bramble; a shrubbery
part. adj. (f. -ī), Withered, faded away.
part. adj. (f. -ī), Withered, faded, dried up, &c.
s.f. (dialec.), A thorny shrub, a bush; bushy place, thicket.
v.t. To weed; — to wipe, clean and plaster (a house).
s.f. The shivering or ague that precedes fever, incipient fever.
adj. Cut small or thin, pared; lopped (as a branch); emaciated, reduced (in body); — a bundle of small twigs ( = jhunkaṭ).
s.m. A dense mass, a crowd, multitude, large gathering or muster, assembly, ball; array (of troops), manœuvres (of troops); battle, conflict; a shawl twisted round the head as a turban.

ety: jhur˚ = S. जुर(ति), rt. जुर्; or S. क्षर(ति), rt. क्षर्
def: v.n. To waste away, to grow thin; to pine (from grief), to wither, fade.

ety: jhūr˚ = S. जूर्य(ति), rt. जुर् or जीर्य(ति), rt. ज्ऋ; cf. also rt. झ्ऋ
def: v.n. To pine, fade, wither, dry up ( = murjhānā).
s.f. A wrinkle, fold, pucker.
s.m. = H جهوسي झूसी jhūsī, s.f. Fine rain, misle, drizzle ( = jhīsī).
s.f. = H جهوسا झूसा jhūsā, s.m. Fine rain, misle, drizzle ( = jhīsī).
adj. & s.m. False, &c. = jhūṭ, jhūṭh, q.v.
= H جهوٿا झूटा jhūṭā = jhūṭh, and jhūṭhā, qq.v.
= H جهوٿ झूट jhūṭ = jhūṭh, and jhūṭhā, qq.v.
s.m.f. Left, rejected, stale; — a stale thing; a cast-off woman; leavings, &c. ( = jhūṭā).
v.t. = jhuṭālnā, q.v.

ety: denom. fr. jhūṭā, q.v.; āl or lā = caus. affix
def: v.t. To pollute victuals by touching or tasting; to touch or taste (food); to leave (remains of food); — jhuṭālnā, v.t. To assert falsely, to falsify, to belie; to prove (a statement, &c.) to be false, to disprove; to prove (one) to be in the wrong; to give (one) the lie ( = jhūṭā karnā)
subs: v.n. To taste food, to eat something
To give (one) the lie to his face.

def: s.m. = jhūṭhan, q.v.

ety: denom. fr. jhūṭā, q.v.; āl or lā = caus. affix
def: v.t. To pollute victuals by touching or tasting; to touch or taste (food); to leave (remains of food); — jhuṭālnā, v.t. To assert falsely, to falsify, to belie; to prove (a statement, &c.) to be false, to disprove; to prove (one) to be in the wrong; to give (one) the lie ( = jhūṭā karnā)
subs: v.n. To taste food, to eat something
To give (one) the lie to his face.

def: s.m. Remains or leavings of food, orts, refuse, offal; that which has touched food and is thereby defiled; — defilement, pollution ( = jhūṭhā); — a lie, a falsehood; — adj. Lying, false, &c. (see jhūṭhā)
subs: To frame or invent a lie
To tell a lie, to lie; lying, uttering a falsehood
(-kā), To detect falsehood; to convict (one) of a lie
To regard or consider as a lie, to disbelieve
(-ko), To say what is false (of), to lie (against), to calumniate
s.m. Lie, lying, falsehood; — adv. Falsely, deceptively, feignedly; vainly, to no purpose, uselessly, needlessly; without ground or cause, without rhyme or reason.

def: s.m. Leavings of food, &c. = jhūṭh, q.v.; — adj. (f. -ī), Touched (by food), defiled; — lying, false; pretended, feigned, insincere, sham, hypocritical; groundless, imaginary; supposititious; delusive, illusive, vain; unsound, invalid; fictitious, invented, artificial, forged (as a document); counterfeit, not genuine; not pure, alloyed, base (as coin); failing of power, untrustworthy, useless (as a limb, a tool, &c.); — s.m. A false person, a liar
subs: jhūṭhī, adv. Falsely, feignedly, hypocritically, in pretence, by way of sham, as a make-believe, as a mere matter of form
(-ko), To falsify, to belie; to prove false; to convict (one) of falsehood
v.n. To be proved false; to be convicted of lying; — to prove false or untrustworthy, to fail (one), to be powerless or useless (as a limb, a tool or weapon, &c.)
jhūṭā-jhāṭā, s.m. Left food, offal, &c. = jhūṭhā, jhūṭh, q.v.
To eat leavings; to be very wretched
jhūṭhā kāgaẕ, s.m. A false or forged document

def: s.f. Falseness, untrueness, untruthfulness, &c.
v.t. = jhuṭālnā, q.v.
s.f. = jhūṭhan, jūtiyāl, &c. qq.v.
s.f. Land yielding a double crop yearly ( = jhūṭhālī, jūṭiyāṅ, jhuṭhāt, jhūṭhiyāʼil, jhūṅṭhar).
s.m. pl. (Braj pl. of jhūṭh). Leavings of food, orts, refuse ( = jhūṭh).
s.f. = jhūṭhan, q.v.
v.t. = jhuṭālnā, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) A lie, &c. = jhūṭh, q.v.
v.t. = jhuṭālnā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = jhūṭhā, q.v.
adv. In pretence, in fun or sport; for love (opp. to 'for money').

s.m. Life, soul, self, spirit, mind; heart; courage; disposition; affection, regard; strength, health; — any living thing; — a form of address, a term of endearment or respect, Sir, Master, Madam; — adv. Yes Sir, yes; true; very good ( = jī hāṅ)
subs: (-par), To have the mind suddenly fixed (on a thing or person); to have the heart set (on), to long (for); to fall in love (with)
(-kī t̤araj-se), To withdraw one's affection, or friendship, or desire (from)
v.n. To be dazed, be bewildered, be confounded
(-se), To be weary (of), be tired or disgusted
v.n. To lose one's mind, reason, or senses
To vomit, to feel nausea; to give offence (to, -kā), to displease, to grieve; to take offence, be displeased
(apnā), To be moderately desirous; to cheer or comfort (oneself); to inspire (another) with courage, rouse the spirit (of, -kā), to animate, encourage; to raise the spirits (of), to cheer
v.n. The heart to sink; to sink; to faint
v.n. The heart to be full, to be touched with compassion, be deeply moved; to be seized with grief
(-se), To be contented, be at ease; to be full, be satiated
v.n. To divert the mind (of), to amuse; to amuse oneself, to dissipate reflection
(-kā), To get at or to understand one's temper or disposition; to know the pleasure or desire (of); — to be highly pleased, to get new life; to escape from misfortune
(-kā), To vex or try one exceedingly, to worry, tease, harass, plague
(-ke), To act according to the pleasure or wish (of), to obey (one)
v.n. To sport with one's life, to risk one's life, to run the hazard ( = jān-par khelnā)
(-meṅ), To be a mass of life, to become maggoty, be full of worms
jī pighalnā or pighal-jānā, v.n. The heart to melt, to be deeply moved, to feel pity or compassion; to feel affection
v.n. The heart to give way, to be broken-hearted
(-se), To turn (from), to be contented, be satiated; to be disgusted (with); to feel nausea
The heart alone knows: jī jalānā (-kā), To inflame or vex the soul (o), to wound or grieve, to plague; to rouse emotion (of gratitude, &c.) in the heart (of), to befriend
v.n. To be vexed or troubled in mind, to have the heart inflamed, or wounded or grieved
If you wish, if you like
(-se), To try to escape (from work, &c.), to shirk or neglect (work), to do (a work) carelessly or lazily; — to pretend to be dead
adj. Brave, courageous; generous, liberal; crazy, daft, out of (one's) mind
(-par), To long (for), hanker (after), to covet; to put forth spirit or courage, to act bravely, to brave, venture, dare
(-par), To feel desire (for), to long (for); — to lose one's senses, become distracted in mind, go mad
(-se), To try to escape (from work), to shirk (work, = jī ćurānā)
v.n. To lose heart, to be disheartened or discouraged; to be faint, to sink
adj. Possessing spirit, brave, courageous, enterprising
s.m. Granting life; pardon (of a capital crime); quarter; permission; — jī-dān karnā (-kā), To grant life, to pardon (a capital crime)
v.n. To be troubled in mind or heart, to be distressed, sorrowful, &c.
v.n. To have palpitation of the heart; to shake, shudder
v.n. To faint, to sink ( = jī bikharnā); to be lost in meditation
v.n. To have a mind (to, -kā); to please, be easily pleased; to get at the heart (of), to please, charm, captivate
(-ke), To lose the regard or favour (of), to fall in the esteem (of); — to lose all heart, to be broken-hearted
v.n. To lose life, give up life, to die
(-ko), To kill ( = mār-ḍālnā), to deprive of life
(-kā), To set the heart (on), to desire, long (for)
(-ke), To touch the heart, to be approved or acceptable; to wound, hurt
(apnā), To mortify desire, to repress a longing, to practise self-denial
(-kā or apnā), To wear out (one's) life, spend oneself, to work to death
adv. Freely, without restraint or stint; to (one's) heart's content; with pleasure, cheerfully
To ask that one's life be spared; to seek or request pardon; to preface a discourse with excuses
To devote one's life for another; to espouse the cause of another
v.n. To remain unsatisfied (a wish or longing)
v.n. To be uneasy or disturbed in mind, to be agitated, be bewildered
(-se), To turn (from), be weary (of), be disgusted (with), to detest
(-kā), To allure or entice; to captivate the heart (of), to fascinate
(-meṅ or -par), To fix or set the mind (on); to pay attention (to); to excite desire or love; to fall in love (with)
(-meṅ or -par), The heart to be set or fixed (on), to be fond (of), to take or incline (to), to feel affection (for), to be enamoured (of)
(-par), To be eagerly desirous (of), to hanker (after)
(-par), To be very fond (of), to desire eagerly; to flutter, pant (for)
(-kā), To ascertain the mind or views (of), to penetrate the thoughts (of); to captivate the heart (of); to excite one's desire
adj. Capital (as a crime); mortifying one's desires; — s.m. One who mortifies his desires; a destroyer of life, slayer, murderer
(-kā), To destroy life; to mortify one's desire, &c. ( = jī-ko mārnā), q.v.; to cross (one), to vex, displease
v.n. To feel nausea, to be sick at stomach
(-se), To unite in feeling, &c. (with), to contract friendship (with)
(-se), The heart to be attached (to), to agree (with), be in accord (with), to be friends (with)
(-kā), To come into the mind (of), to occur (to one); to have a mind or fancy (for) — jī-meṅ baiṭhnā, v.n. To be impressed on or fixed in the mind
v.n. To be consumed with jealousy, or envy, &c.
v.n. To receive new life, to be revived, be refreshed, to be comforted
(-ke), To impart one's feelings or convictions (to another), to convince, persuade
(-ko), To bear or keep in mind; to keep to oneself, to keep secret
v.n. To go home to one's heart; to be struck or taken with, to take the fancy (of)
(-ke), To find a place in one's heart, to make oneself agreeable or acceptable (to), to win one's regard, to please
v.n. To expire, die; to be dying for, (-par), to desire or love excessively; to be in mortal fear (of)
v.n. To lose heart, to become faint-hearted, be spiritless, be discouraged or depressed (from fear)
(-se), The heart or mind to turn away (from), to become averse (to), have an aversion for, to dislike
(-kā), To have a desire or wish (for)
adv. In one's inmost soul; — to oneself
rām-kā jī, s.m. A pure and simple mind; — a simpleton, an innocent.

def: s.f. The tongue ( = jībh, q.v.)
s.m. Tax, tribute; revenue.
s.m. pl. (of jabha), Foreheads.
s.m. = jīwak, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of jabal), Mountains, hills.
adj. (f. -ī), Having a tougue, tongued (used in comp.)
subs: adj. & s.m. Black-tongued; — a black-tongued person, one whose curses are effective.

def: s.f. = jībhī, q.v.
s.f. = jībhī, q.v.
s.f. The tongue
subs: v.n. 'The tongue to turn back'; to pronounce distinctly
v.n. 'To make the tongue long'; to be loquacious and abusive; to pursue pleasures beyond one's reach
(-kī), To silence; to interrupt the discourse (of anyone); to criticize minutely
v.n. To pretend to wealth falsely
(-par), To smack the tongue (over or at), to long (for something unattainable), to covet
(-par), To boast (of) beyond one's ability; to talk grandly or largely
v.n. To speak hesitatingly or with reserve

(-kī), To stop one's tongue by signs, to forbid by signs; — (from the Persian) to grant the request (of a petitioner); to bite the tongue in astonishment, &c.; to be struck with terror or astonishment
(prov.), To set the tongue agoing, to speak, to answer; to be rude or insolent in speech, to abuse
To put out the tongue; to be extremely fatigued or thirsty; to pull out the tongue (as a punishment for misusing it).

ety: Prk. जिब्भालो and जिब्भालओ; S. जिव्हा+आलः and आल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Plain-spoken; loquacious, long-tongued; foul-tongued, abusive; — a loquacious person, &c.
adj. Talkative, loquacious; plain-spoken, long-tongued; foul-tongued, abusive; — s.m. A loquacious person, &c.

ety: Prk. जिब्भालो and जिब्भालओ; S. जिव्हा+आलः and आल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Plain-spoken; loquacious, long-tongued; foul-tongued, abusive; — a loquacious person, &c.
s.f. An instrument for cleaning the tongue, a tongue-scraper; — bit (of a bridle).
adj. Of or pertaining to the tongue; tongue-like, tongue-shaped
adj. & s.f. = jībhī, q.v.
s.f. = jīvikā, q.v.

ety: for A. جبلة, fr. جبل 'to create'
def: s.f. Nature, natural constitution, natural disposition or temper; original quality or property; temperament; essence; idiosyncrasy; creation, form.
adj. Of, or relating to, the natural, innate, or original constitution, disposition, temper, or other quality or property; natural, innate, constitutional, essential (syn. t̤abīʼī).
s.m. (?) The neck; a long, slender, beautiful neck.

ety: inf. n. iii of جدل 'to make firm or strong,' &c.
def: s.m. Contending; contention, altercation, contest.
s.m. Striving, labour, toil; exertion, effort, endeavour, trial, painstaking, study
subs: vulg. jadd-o-jihad, s.m. = jidd; — jidd-o-jahd karnā, To strive, labour, endeavour, &c.
vulg. jadd-o-kadd, s.m. Endeavour, effort, &c.; industry; expostulation

ety: Prk. जेद्दह+र (for रे loc. aff.); S. यावत्
def: adv. (correl. tidhar, udhar), Whither, where, wherever, withersoever; there
subs: adv. Here and there, hither and there, everywhere, all about, in all directions
jidhar niwān udhar pānī ḍhaltā hai
'Where there is a declivity thither flows the water'; misfortunes never come singly; evil unites with evil.

def: adv. (chiefly poet.) = jidhar, q.v.
s.m. A carcass, a corpse.
s.m. An ornament worn on the turban (it consists of a band of velvet about six inches long and two broad, beautifully embroidered, and a gold plate set with precious stones sewn on it).

ety: Pehl. jegar; Zend yakare; S. yakr̤it
def: s.m. The liver; the vitals; the heart; mind; spirit, courage, pluck; kernel, core; (met.) dear, darling; a son; near and dear relations or friends

adj. Heart-burnt; in love; in a fever
Mine is mine and yours is yours, 'Everyone knows where his own shoe pinches'
jigar-ḵẖẉār, s.m. An enchanter, magician, sorcerer
adj. Heart-inflaming, tormenting; moving; heart-inflamed, tormented, troubled in mind
jigar-kā ṭukra, s.m. A piece of the liver; (met.) a son
s.m. A lobe of the liver; (met.), one dear or beloved, a darling.

def: adj. Belonging to the liver or heart; hepatic; liver-coloured; livid; — dear, beloved, intimate, close (friend).
s.f. An exclamation expressive of surprise and pleasure.
s.f. Desire of gaining or obtaining; desire of conquering, military ardour; wish to excel, emulation, rivalry.

ety: by redupl. fr. intens. of rt. gam
def: s.f. Making a fuss (with), caressing, coaxing; wearisome or importunate begging and beseeching; fawning, cringing; flattery, wheedling (syn. lapaṭ-sapaṭ); — jigjigī karnā, v.t. To coax; to importune; to wheedle; to fawn upon, to flatter.
adj. & s.m. Fawning, flattering, wheedling; — one who fawns, a flatterer, a wheedler.
s.f. Desire of knowing, asking, inquiring; inquiry, investigation; inquisitiveness.
adj. Desirous of knowing, inquiring, inquisitive, curious; — s.m. An inquirer, &c.
part. adj. To be inquired into, deserving or requiring investigation; admitting of investigation.
s.m. Power, strength; spirit, courage, boldness, pluck.
s.m. A species of crane.
s.f. = jignāsā, q.v.
s.f. The tongue ( = jībh, the com. form).
s.m. = jī = jīv, q.v.

ety: inf. n. iii of جہد 'to labour,' &c.
def: s.m. A war waged by Muslims against infidels; a crusade.

def: s.m. One who fights against unbelievers, a crusader.

s.f. = jijjī, and jijiyā, q.v.
s.f. A breast, a dug; an elder sister (a term of respect, often applied generally to any elderly female); — jijjī, jijīmāṅ, s.f. Foster-mother, wet-nurse.
s.f. A breast, a dug; an elder sister (a term of respect, often applied generally to any elderly female); — jijjī, jijīmāṅ, s.f. Foster-mother, wet-nurse.

part. adj. Deserving of being inquired into, needing or calling for investigation, admitting of investigation.
s.m. contrac. of jīvikā, q.v.
s.f. A high note or tone (in music), the treble (cf. jhīl); the treble-sounding member (of the tabor, &c.).

subs: (-ko), To impart lustre (to), to grace, adorn

def: v.t. To give life to, cause to live, to vivify, animate; to restore to life; to allow to live, to preserve alive; to foster, to patronize
subs: v.t. To restore to life; to revive.

def: s.f. The skin; hide, leather; binding (of a book), a volume, book
adj. Of or pertaining to the skin, cutaneous.
s.m. A companion, comrade, mess-mate, guest.

ety: also jem = Prk. जेम, जेम्व; fr. S. यावत
def: conj. (old H.) Like, &c. = jaisā and jyoṅ.

r: jīm, Urdū, Hindūstānī, Nāgarī, j, judge, abjad, jamādaʼl-āḵẖir
def: The seventh letter of the Urdū or Hindūstānī, and the eighth consonant of the Nāgarī alphabet. It has the sound of the English j as in judge. In the abjad, q.v., it has the numerical value of 3. In almanacs it stands for Tuesday; in astronomy for the sign Cancer. It may also stand as an abbreviation for the Arabic month jamādaʼl-āḵẖir.
v.n. To copulate

ety: Prk. जिमाव(इ) or जिम्माव˚ = S. जेम+आपय(ति), rt. जिम्
def: v.t. (caus. of jemnā), To feed, to entertain
s.f. A feast, &c. = jeʼonār, q.v.
adj. See s.v. jī.

ety: jīm˚ = Prk. जिम्म(इ) and जेम(इ); S. जेम(ति), rt. जिम्
def: v.n. To eat, to take food; — to take (a bribe); to embezzle.

ety: Prk. जवड (by change of ड to ण), fr. S. यावत्
def: The formative, or base of the oblique cases, of the pl. of the rel. pron. jo, 'who,' &c.

s.m. A generic term applied to a Buddh or chief saint of the Bauddh sect, in the same manner as to a Jain saint; a Jin, a generic name of the personage peculiar to the Jain sect who is ranked by them as superior to the gods of the other sects; a deified saint and teacher.
adj. Old, decayed, &c.; — an old man (see jīrṇa).
adv. (dialec.) No, not; (prohibitive) do not (syn. mat).
s.m. corr. of zīn, q.v.

ety: jī˚ = Prk. जीअ(इ) = S. जीव(ति), rt. जीव्
def: v.n. To live, be alive, to exist, to subsist; to enjoy life; to be revived, be restored (to health, &c.); — s.m. Living; life
subs: v.n. To rise up alive, to be resuscitated, to come back to life again, to be revived, to get new life.

def: s.f. pl. (of jannat), Gardens (planted with trees); the regions of paradise.

ety: for جناية, inf. n. of جني
def: s.f. Any prohibited injurious action, as a wounding, maiming, &c.

subs: adj. & s.m. Drawing a bier; — drawer of a bier.

s.f. Specification; classification; — adv. Specifically; separately, distinctly

s.m. A generic or indeterminate noun

def: adj. (rel. n. fr. jins, q.v.), Generic, generical; in kind, in goods (paid, &c.).

s.m. Cumin-seed ( = P. zīra).

s.f. Old age, infirmity, decay: digestion.

adv. To what degree or extent; as much as; whatever, whatsoever

def: adj. Victorious, triumphant; conquering, winning, gaining; excelling; — s.m. The sun; an epithet of Indra, of Vishnu, and of Arjun; name of a Vasu; name of a son of Manu Bhautya; name of the father of Brahma-gupta.

adj. Bodily, corporeal; personal.
s.f. Bodiliness, corporeity, materiality.
s.m. A bridge (syn. pul).

ety: Prk. जेत्तहे, loc. sing. of जेत्तिअ = S. यावत्
def: adv. (Braj., correl. to tit), Where, wheresoever.

ety: Prk. जित्ता; जित्या, rt. जि; or v.n. of jītnā, q.v.

subs: s.m. Record of victory or triumph (furnished by the defeated party in a wrestling match, a learned disputation, &c.); a favourable decree (in litigation)
adj. & s.m. Conquering, overcoming; winning; — victor; winner
v.n. To obtain a victory, to achieve success.

ety: Prk. जीवंतओ; S. जीवन्त+कः; or imperf. part. of jīnā
def: adj. (f. -ī), Living, alive
subs: adj. Living and healthy; alive and well, alive and flourishing
v.n. To remain or continue alive; to enjoy a long life
adj. & adv. All alive, quite alive (as I am, or he is, &c.; e.g. majhe jītā-hī zamīn-kā paiwand kar-do)
adv. Living, alive, in the living flesh; in the life-time (of, -ke); for life, during the lifetime (of -ke)
v.n. To suffer death in life, to die a living death; to endure extreme misery or anguish.

def: s.m. Mutual assistance in tillage; allowing the use of a plough and bullocks instead of paying wages in money or kind ( = jīterā).
adj. Over, above, more, in excess
subs: v.n. To be over, to exceed, be in excess.

def: part. Conquered, vanquished, subdued; overcome, subject (to), enslaved (by); won, gained, obtained
subs: adj. & s.m. Self-subdued, void of passion; — one who has brought his passions under control
jitendriyā (jita+indr˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Having the senses or passions subdued, calm, impassive; — one who has couquered his passions or subdued the senses; an ascetic, a sage; a chaste person
s.f. Subjugation of the senses or passions; self-restraint, continence, chastity
adj. & s.m. Of subdued anger, not to be roused to wrath, imperturbable; — one whose anger has been brought under control.

def: s.m. Life, existence ( = jīvan).
s.m. Arrow-root.
v.t. To cause to conquer or win.
s.m. = jīt-patr, q.v.s.v. jīt.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. jita.
s.m. = jītav, q.v.
s.m. = jītab, q.v.
adj. dialec. var. of jitnā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. jita, jit.
s.m. Mutual help in tillage, &c. ( = jītā, q.v.).
s.f. The husband's elder brother's wife (see jeṭh).
s.f. Name of a fast observed by Hindū women who have lost children, with the view of preserving the remainder. (In this fast the goddess Devī is sacrificed to and worshipped)
adj. & s.m. See s.v. jita, jit.
v.t. = jitānā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī; correl. itnā; utnā, titnā), As much as; as many; how much; however much
subs: adv. In as much (time) as; inasmuch as; on which.

ety: Prk. जित्त(इ) or जित्ते(इ), fr. S. जीत, p.p.p. of rt. ज्या
def: v.t. To win; to conquer, vanquish, overpower, overcome, defeat, beat; to subdue; to suppress; — v.n. To be victorious (over, -se); to succeed
v.n. To be conquered or subdued; to be won.
adj. (f. -ī), As much, as many, &c. ( = jitnā, q.v.)
s.m. = jītwaiyā, q.v.
s.m. A winner, victor ( = jīt-want, q.v.s.v. jīt).

ety: for jitawāwnā, doub. caus. of jītnā; ˚wā˚ = ˚awāw˚ = Prk. आवे or आव = S. आपि redupl.
def: v.t. To cause to be overcome; to cause to conquer or win ( = jitānā).
adj. & s.m. Victorious, triumphant; a conqueror, victor.
s.f. Victrix, an epithet of the city of Benares.
part. adj. (f. -ā), To be overcome; capable of being overcome, conquerable, invincible.

s.m. Life, existence; soul; living creature, animate being, animal; the sentient individual soul (or emanation of the Deity, which, it is believed, is incorporated in the animal body, imparting to it life, motion, and sensation, and hence called jīvātman, in contradistinction to paramātman or supreme soul); — a beloved one, darling, sweetheart; — intj. Bravo!
subs: (S. jīva+āt˚), s.m. The individual soul, the vital principle or spirit
(jīva+ādhāra), s.m. The earth, the world of organic beings
(jīva+an˚), s.m. Destroyer of life, a slayer, murderer; a fowler, bird-catcher
s.m. A plant that bears seeds of which rosaries are made
or jīvan-jīvika, s.m. A kind of bird (supposed to be a pheasant); the chakor or Greek partridge
s.m. Giving life; rescuing from sickness; — title of a manual of medicine composed by Ćyavana
adj. & s.m. Life-giving; — who or what gives life
s.f. Kindness or tenderness for animal life
s.m. The appropriate functions of animal life
s.m. Living property, live stock, wealth in flocks and herds
s.f. The mother of living offspring
(-ke), To devote or sacrifice one's life for another; to espouse the cause of another
s.f. A house or hospital for vermin, &c. (such as may be seen at Surat)
jīʼū-gar, adj. Lively, spirited; brave, courageous, bold, resolute ( = jīvaṭ)
s.m. 'The mansion of the soul,' the body
s.f. Injury to life, destruction of life.

def: s.m. corr. of jīv, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑁄 (jo), from Sanskrit यः (yáḥ), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *Hyás, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hyás, from Proto-Indo-European *Hyós.

def: s.f. The chord of an are, &c. = jyā, q.v.
s.f. Land assigned as subsistence (to relations or dependants), rent-free land.
s.m. See s.v. jīv.
s.m. A living being, living creature, animal
subs: the tree Pentaptera tomentosa; name of a medicinal plant
(considered as one of the eight principal drugs classed together under the name ashṭa-varg).

def: s.m. pl. (of jūl), The walls or enclosures of wells, parapets (of wells).
s.m. Life, existence; subsistence; means of subsistence, living, livelihood, profession; — water; — a son
subs: s.m. Maintenance; stipend allowed to the family of an old servant deceased
jīvan-dāyak, adj. & s.m. Life-giving; — giver of life, an epithet of the Deity
jīvan-mūri, s.m. The root of life (a term of endearment)
s.m. Acquitting (oneself) of the obligations of life
jīvan-vān, jīvan-vant, adj. (f. -vatī), Possessed of life; pertaining or relating to life
s.m. A life-giving or invigorating medicine, a drug for reviving the dead, elixir vitœ
adj. Endowed with life, living, animate.

def: v.t. = jimānā, q.v.
v.t. To cause to live (see jilānā).
s.f. A kind of jasmine.
adj. & s.m. Living, alive; — life; a living creature.
s.f. The plants Celtis orientalis; Menispermum glabrum; the Sam or Saen tree, Mimosa albida; the yellow myrobalan, Terminalia chebula; and other plants.
s.m. A fowler, bird-catcher.
s.f. A parasitical plant, Epidendron tesseloides, and other species; the plant, Menispermum glabrum.
adj. Living, alive; for life, during life; lively, spirited, &c. = jīwaṭ, q.v.; — s.m. corr. of jīvit, q.v.
subs: s.f. A woman whose husband is alive
s.f. A woman whose children are living
s.m. A subsistence for life; a life-tenure of rent-free laud
(i.e. jīvat+mukta), adj. & s.m. Exonerated whilst living from future birth or transmigration and all ritual ceremonies; — one who is so exonerated

s.f. Liberation in the present state of life; (in the Vedānta system) a secondary sort of liberation which conducts the soul after death to live with Brahma, not, however, divested of a subtile corporeal frame.

def: adj. Lively, spirited; brave, courageous, bold, resolute; strong, vigorous, powerful.
s.f. The state of life or existence.
s.m. Life, existence; a medicine for restoring life.
adj. = jīwaṭ, q.v.
s.f. = jihvā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Living, alive, animate; living (upon or by); — a living being.
s.f. Means of subsistence, livelihood, maintenance, allowance, stipend, pension; occupation or profession (by which a subsistence is obtained).
adj. & s.m. Living, alive, existent — living, life, existence; duration of life; livelihood, &c. ( = jīvan)
subs: s.m. Life-time, period or duration of life, a life
(jīvita+īśā), s.m. 'The lord or master of existence'; a lover; a husband; an epithet of Yama the regent of death; a drug to revive the dead.
